messages.content:
You are a question answering assistant. Your job is to generate a SPARQL query to answer a given user question.

You should follow a step-by-step approach to generate the SPARQL query:
1. Determine possible entities and properties implied by the user question.
2. Search for the entities and properties in the knowledge graphs. Where applicable, constrain the searches with already identified entities and properties.
3. Gradually build up the SPARQL query using the identified entities and properties. Start with simple queries and add more complexity as needed. Execute intermediate queries to get feedback and to verify your assumptions. You may need to refine or rethink your current plan based on the query results and go back to step 2 if needed, possibly multiple times.
4. Use the answer or cancel function to finalize your answer and stop the generation process.

Available knowledge graphs:
- "wikidata" at https://qlever.dev/api/wikidata
  - "entity" index (type="fuzzy"): Entities indexed by their labels and synonyms
  - "property" index (type="embedding", modalities="text"): Properties indexed by their labels, synonyms, and identifiers

Index types used:
- "embedding": Retrieves items by cosine similarity between their embeddings and the query embedding. The embedding model used depends on the index and may support text, images, or both.
- "fuzzy": Retrieves items by overlap between their label words and the query keywords. The query keywords must not match label words exactly, but some fuzziness is allowed. The longer a query keyword is, the more it can deviate from a label word and still be considered a match, though it will also contribute less to the overall score. No special query operators like AND/OR are supported.

SPARQL prefixes for use in function calls:
- bd: http://www.bigdata.com/rdf#
- cc: http://creativecommons.org/ns#
- data: https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Special:EntityData/
- dc: http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/
- dct: http://purl.org/dc/terms/
- foaf: http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/
- geo: http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#
- geof: http://www.opengis.net/def/function/geosparql/
- gn: http://www.geonames.org/ontology#
- hint: http://www.bigdata.com/queryHints#
- imdb: https://www.imdb.com/
- math: http://www.w3.org/2005/xpath-functions/math#
- ontolex: http://www.w3.org/ns/lemon/ontolex#
- owl: http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#
- p: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/
- pq: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/
- pqn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/value-normalized/
- pqv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/value/
- pr: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/
- prn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/value-normalized/
- prov: http://www.w3.org/ns/prov#
- prv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/value/
- ps: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/
- psn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/value-normalized/
- psv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/value/
- qb: http://purl.org/linked-data/cube#
- qfn: http://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/function#
- ql: http://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/builtin-functions/
- qlss: https://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/spatialSearch/
- rdf: http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#
- rdfs: http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#
- ref: http://www.wikidata.org/reference/
- s: http://www.wikidata.org/entity/statement/
- schema: http://schema.org/
- skos: http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#
- v: http://www.wikidata.org/value/
- void: http://rdfs.org/ns/void#
- wd: http://www.wikidata.org/entity/
- wdno: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/novalue/
- wdt: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct/
- wdtn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct-normalized/
- wikibase: http://wikiba.se/ontology#
- xsd: http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#

Additional rules to follow:
- Explain your thought process before and after each step and function call.
- Do not ask the user for clarification until you have made an initial attempt at completing the task. When the task input is incomplete or ambiguous, proceed based on reasonable assumptions.
- Do not use "SERVICE wikibase:label { bd:serviceParam wikibase:language ..." in SPARQL queries. It is unsupported by the used QLever SPARQL endpoints. Use rdfs:label or similar properties to get labels instead.
- Always execute your final SPARQL query before giving an answer to make sure it returns the expected results.
- The SPARQL query should always return the actual identifiers / IRIs of the items in its result. It additionally may return labels or other human-readable information, but they are optional and should be put within optional clauses unless explicitly requested by the user.
- Do not stop early if there are still obvious improvements to be made to the SPARQL query. For example, keep refining your SPARQL query if its result contains irrelevant items or is missing items you expected.
- Do not perform additional computation (e.g. filtering, sorting, calculations) on the result of the SPARQL query to determine the answer. All computation should be done solely within SPARQL.
- For questions with a "True" or "False" answer the SPARQL query should be an ASK query.
How many terms were served that were by the party with members in the Rajya Sabha from Maharashtra since 1952 once led by Ghandi

content.message:
I need to find how many terms were served by the party that had members in the Rajya Sabha from Maharashtra since 1952, with a connection to Gandhi leading that party.

Let me break this down step by step:

1. First, I need to identify the political party associated with Gandhi. Gandhi was primarily associated with the Indian National Congress.

2. Then, I need to find when the Indian National Congress had members in the Rajya Sabha from Maharashtra since 1952.

3. Finally, I need to count how many terms were served by this party.

Let me start by searching for the party associated with Gandhi:

tool_calls.name: search_entity
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 entity alternatives:
1. Indian National Congress (wd:Q10225), also known as "Congress", "Congress Party", "INC", "Indian Congress Party":
  - centrist-liberal political party in India
  - is instance of political party
2. Indian National Congress (wd:Q3523002):
  - is instance of political party
  - political party in India, active 1969–77
3. Indian National Congress (wd:Q117206660):
  - is instance of Wikimedia disambiguation page
  - Wikimedia disambiguation page
4. Indian National Congress (wd:L1320299-F1)
5. Category:Indian National Congress (wd:Q7283636):
  - is instance of Wikimedia category
  - Wikimedia category
6. Template:Indian National Congress (wd:Q12014343):
  - is instance of Wikimedia template
  - Wikimedia template
7. Indian National Congress (R) (wd:Q6021036), also known as "Congress (R)", "Indian National Congress (Requisition)", "Indian National Congress (Requisitionists)":
  - Indian political party
  - is instance of political party
8. Indian National Congress (U) (wd:Q6021043), also known as "Indian National Congress (Urs)":
  - Indian political party
  - is instance of political party
9. Indian National Congress (Jagjivan) (wd:Q16937251):
  - is instance of political party
  - political party in India

content.message: The Indian National Congress (wd:Q10225) is the most relevant and well-known party associated with Mahatma Gandhi. Let me verify this by checking the properties of this entity.
I need to find more information about the Indian National Congress to confirm its connection to Gandhi. Let me search for relationships between Mahatma Gandhi and the Indian National Congress.

tool_calls.name: search_with_constraints
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:
1. NDL Authority ID (wd:P349 as wdtn/wdt/p, matched via "ndl"), also known as "National Diet Library Auth ID", "National Diet Library ID", "National Diet Library of Japan authority control ID", "National Diet Library of Japan ID", "ndl", etc.:
  - identifier for authority control per the National Diet Library of Japan
2. different from (wd:P1889 as wdt/p, matched via "not"), also known as "confused with", "different than", "different to", "differs from", "diff from", etc.:
  - item that is different from another item, with which it may be confused
3. Open Library ID (wd:P648 as wdtn/wdt/p, matched via "OLID"), also known as "OL ID", "OLID", "Open Library author ID", "OpenLibrary author ID", "Open Library book ID", etc.:
  - identifier for a work ("W"), edition ("M") or author ("A") for book data of the Internet Archive
4. child (wd:P40 as wdt/p, matched via "son"), also known as "children", "daughter", "daughters", "descendants", "father of", etc.:
  - subject has object as child. Do not use for stepchildren—use "relative" (P1038), qualified with "kinship to subject" (P1039)
5. medical condition (wd:P1050 as wdt/p, matched via "condition"), also known as "ailment", "condition", "disability", "disease", "disorder", etc.:
  - any state relevant to the health of an organism, including diseases and positive conditions
6. described by source (wd:P1343 as wdt/p, matched via "entry"), also known as "dbs", "def in", "defined in", "desc by biography", "desc by encyclopedia", etc.:
  - work where this item is described, in statistical contexts, a methodological note describing the data
7. relative (wd:P1038 as wdt/p), also known as "ancestor", "aunt", "aunt-in-law", "brother-in-law", "co-brother-in-law", etc.:
  - family member (qualify with "kinship to subject", P1039; for direct family member please use specific property)
8. image (wd:P18 as wdt/p, matched via "still"), also known as "album artwork", "album cover", "artwork", "camera picture", "Commons image", etc.:
  - image of relevant illustration of the subject; if available, ... map, flag image, signature image, logo image, collage image)
9. languages spoken, written or signed (wd:P1412 as wdt/p, matched via "language"), also known as "language", "language of expression", "language read", "language signed", "languages of expression", etc.:
  - language(s) that a person or a people speaks, writes or signs, including the native language(s)
10. Wikimedia outline (wd:P5125 as wdt/p, matched via "outline"), also known as "outline":
  - item about an outline of the topic at Wikimedia sites

tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:
1. has effect (wd:P1542 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "led to"), also known as "caused", "cause of", "causes", "consequence", "effect", etc.:

2. organization directed by the office or position (wd:P2389 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "leads"), also known as "chairman of", "chair of", "chairperson of", "chairwoman of", "commander of", etc.:
  - organization or project which is directed by this office
3. position held by head of the organization (wd:P2388 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "led by"), also known as "chairperson's postion", "chief's position", "directed by", "directed by / from", "director", etc.:
  - position of the head of this item
4. perpetrator (wd:P8031 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "lead by"), also known as "accomplice", "agressor", "assailant", "assassin", "attacker", etc.:
  - person or organization who carried out this harmful, illegal, or immoral act
5. point in time (wd:P585 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "on"), also known as "as of", "at time", "by date", "date", "during", etc.:
  - date something took place, existed or a statement was true; for providing time use the "refine date" property (P4241)
6. located in/on physical feature (wd:P706 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "on"), also known as "geographical region", "is in", "is on", "landform", "located on island", etc.:
  - located on the specified (geo)physical feature. Should not be ... range (P4552). Use P206 for things in/on bodies of water.
7. winner (wd:P1346 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "leader"), also known as "1st place medalist", "awardee", "champ", "champion", "has winner", etc.:
  - winner of a competition or similar event, not to be used from ... qualified with "for work" (P1686)) nor for wars or battles
8. chairperson (wd:P488 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "leader"), also known as "chair", "chairman", "chairwoman", "dean", "headed by", etc.:
  - presiding member of an organization, group or body
9. director / manager (wd:P1037 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "leader"), also known as "boss", "chief", "director", "director general", "general manager", etc.:
  - person who manages any kind of group or organisation
10. head of state (wd:P35 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "leader"), also known as "chief of state", "emperor", "governor", "king", "leader", etc.:
  - official with the highest formal authority in a country/state

content.message:
I need to find the property that represents a person being a member of the Rajya Sabha. The most relevant property appears to be "member of" (wdt:P463), which represents membership in an organization or legislative body.

Now I need to find how many terms were served by the Indian National Congress (the party led by Gandhi) in the Rajya Sabha from Maharashtra since 1952. 

I need to:
1. Find the relationship between the Indian National Congress and members of the Rajya Sabha
